symptoms.symptom_name: Widespread well-demarcated dyspigmented (pale and light-brown) macules/patches coalescing over the upper trunk, neck and proximal arms

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The seborrhoeic upper-trunk distribution with confluent dyspigmented macules is the hallmark pattern.
Pale patches occur in both, but vitiligo favours acral/periorificial sites and is scale-free.

symptoms.symptom_name: Affected pale areas fail to tan with sun exposure, becoming more prominent against surrounding tanned skin

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Malassezia-derived azelaic acid inhibits melanocyte tyrosinase, so treated areas do not tan — a fairly specific historical clue.

symptoms.symptom_name: Dramatic flare after a hot, humid tropical holiday with heavy sweating

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Heat, humidity and sweat drive the yeast-to-hyphal conversion and overgrowth — the classic precipitant.

symptoms.symptom_name: Lesions largely asymptomatic with only mild intermittent itch; patient systemically well

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Versicolor is typically asymptomatic or only mildly itchy and never systemically unwell — consistent with a benign superficial process.

symptoms.symptom_name: Fine branny (dust-like) scale released on stretching or lightly scraping the lesions (Besnier's sign)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Superficial branny scale on stretch strongly confirms a scaly superficial dermatosis in the versicolor pattern.
Vitiligo is completely smooth and scale-free; demonstrable scale effectively excludes it. This sign is unavailable via telehealth, so remote exclusion of vitiligo is incomplete.

symptoms.symptom_name: No palmar/plantar rash and no generalised lymphadenopathy

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Palmar/plantar involvement and lymphadenopathy are hallmark of secondary syphilis; their absence makes this mimic very unlikely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Wood's lamp yellow-green fluorescence / KOH microscopy showing hyphae and spores ('spaghetti and meatballs')

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: KOH microscopy is confirmatory for Malassezia; a bedside/lab test never available in real time on telehealth, so the telehealth diagnosis remains presumptive.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Confluent upper-trunk dyspigmentation + failure to tan + heat/sweat trigger together make the benign diagnosis confident enough for presumptive telehealth management.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Supports the diagnosis via characteristic fluorescence at the routine confirmatory review — not required urgently and not obtainable via telehealth.
Confirmatory if the diagnosis is in doubt or disease is recurrent/extensive; a routine in-person/lab step, not an emergency investigation.
There is no systemic illness; ordering urgent investigations or sending the patient for emergency work-up would be over-investigation of a benign superficial condition.
